description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a blue fuselage and red wings, featuring roundels and insignia reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has a high, straight biplane wing configuration with struts and wires connecting the upper and lower wings. Twin piston engines with wooden propellers are mounted between the wings, and the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching red and blue colors. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, suitable for one or two pilots. The landing gear is not clearly visible, but the hull shape suggests it may be a floatplane or flying boat design. The overall appearance is robust and functional, with a hard, painted metal and wood exterior.
description: A vintage blue and red biplane with twin engines, open cockpit, and military roundels, featuring a robust, classic design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, red upper wing, and tail with roundels; cockpit and engine details are visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, twin engines, and symmetrical placement of roundels on the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the blue fuselage, red wing, and tail, with cockpit and engine details.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the twin propellers, open cockpit, and the biplane's stacked wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane with straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings, each with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; classic empennage layout.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float-type hull, suggesting a flying boat or floatplane configuration; no traditional wheel gear visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit at the front of the fuselage, likely single or tandem seat with minimal canopy.
